message:
audit: fcp-protocol clean — framing/handshake/version negotiation hardened

Reviewed fcpc.rs, fcps.rs, session.rs, symbol_envelope.rs.

Framing: strict version equality (no negotiation = no downgrade).
decode_with_limit enforces claimed > max AND bytes.len() == expected
strict-equal — blocks length-prefix spoofing in either direction.
FCPC AAD binds session_id/seq/flags so flag-bit truncation is
AEAD-caught.

FCPS: validate_frame_lengths uses checked arithmetic and binds
symbol_count * record_size + HEADER == bytes.len(), gated first by
bytes.len() <= max_datagram_bytes. TransportLimits.max_datagram_bytes
is u16 (≤65535), bounding Vec::with_capacity(symbol_count) by MTU.
Mutually-exclusive flag combos rejected. Fuzz target exists.

Handshake: decode_canonical_cbor rejects non-canonical encodings AND
trailing bytes. Hello transcript binds suites + transport_limits.
Ack transcript cross-binds hello.eph_pubkey + hello.nonce. Ack verify
checks hello.suites.contains(self.suite) — explicit downgrade defense.
Attestation + identity-NodeId + timestamp-skew checks gate signature
verification.

Symbol envelope: 86-byte per-symbol AAD binds object/zone/epoch/sender
context; HKDF-derived per-sender subkeys; deterministic nonces.

Non-finding for awareness: FCPS frame *headers* (flags, RESPONSE/
STREAMING/ERROR bits) are not in the per-symbol AAD, so flag bits
can be flipped on the wire without breaking individual symbol auth.
Impact bounded to receiver state-machine confusion (spurious
stream-end), not authentication bypass — appears to be by-design for
per-symbol forwarding flexibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
